title: Verify That the System Returns to Running After Bag Out Unless Shutdown Is Chosen
summary: Use this source-based reference to confirm the expected post-bag-out state behavior described in training: once bag out or close out is complete, the system returns to running and remains ready for another sort unless an operator intentionally chooses shutdown.
when_to_use: Use when an operator needs to interpret the expected system state after bag out or close out, verify that the observed state matches the training description, and determine whether the system should remain in running or be intentionally placed into shutdown.
not_for: Detailed troubleshooting of why the system failed to return to running after bag out
A full bag out operating workflow with exact HMI button paths or screen names

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Observe bag out completion, verify the displayed system state, and determine whether shutdown has been intentionally selected.

safety_notes: This runbook is an interpretation and verification reference from training content, not a full control procedure.
Do not assume shutdown occurs automatically at the end of bag out; compare the observed state to the source description.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the system state display or HMI state indication
Source-backed understanding of running, bag out, close out, and shutdown states

access_required: Ability to view the current system state on the system display or HMI
system_preconditions: Bag out or close out has been completed or is believed to have completed
external_references: UPSFORTNA OptiSweep Training Day 1, segment training_video_day1_segment_0021

visual_references.description: Training video frame associated with the discussion of running, bag out or close out, and shutdown states, including the statement that after bag out the system returns to running unless shutdown is chosen.
Lifecycle slide showing the broader system state progression through startup, sorting, bag-out, and shutdown.

steps.title: Identify that bag out or close out has completed
steps.purpose: Establish that the post-bag-out state check is being performed at the correct point in the operating cycle.
steps.instruction: Confirm that the system has finished bag out or close out before interpreting the next state.
steps.expected_result: The operator is checking system state only after bag out or close out completion.
steps.healthy_condition: Bag out or close out is complete and the system is ready for post-cycle state verification.
steps.failure_condition: Bag out or close out is still in progress, so the post-bag-out state cannot yet be confirmed.

actions.label: Confirm bag out completion
actions.exact_action: Identify that bag out or close out has completed.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the training discussion of the distinction between running, bag out or close out, and shutdown states.
Look at the lifecycle framing that includes bag-out as a distinct phase before later state transitions.

stop_or_escalate_if: It is unclear whether bag out or close out has actually completed

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The discussion references startup state, shutdown state, running state, and says “for the most part, you're either running, bagging out, or... close out originally.”
The transcript discusses the distinction between running, bag out or close out, and shutdown states.

steps.title: Check whether the system has returned to running
steps.purpose: Verify the specific source-stated state transition that should occur after bag out completes.
steps.instruction: Check the current system state indication and verify whether it has returned to running after bag out completion.
steps.expected_result: The displayed state shows running after bag out is done.
steps.healthy_condition: The system state indicates running after bag out completion.
steps.failure_condition: The observed state does not indicate running after bag out completion.

actions.label: Verify running state
actions.exact_action: Check the current system state and verify whether it has returned to running, based on the source statement that bag out completion returns the system to running.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the training statement that once bag out is done, the system goes to a running state.

stop_or_escalate_if: The observed state after bag out does not match the documented training description

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: “once it's done with bag out, it will go to a running state”
After bag out, the system automatically returns to running and is ready for another sort.

steps.title: Confirm readiness for another sort unless shutdown was selected
steps.purpose: Interpret the running state correctly as readiness for continued operation unless a separate shutdown choice has been made.
steps.instruction: Confirm that the system is ready for another sort if it has returned to running and no intentional shutdown selection has been made.
steps.expected_result: The operator understands that running after bag out means the system remains ready for another sort.
steps.healthy_condition: The system is in running state and is treated as ready for another sort.
steps.failure_condition: The operator incorrectly assumes the system should already be shut down or not ready for another sort.

actions.label: Interpret readiness state
actions.exact_action: Confirm that the system is ready for another sort unless shutdown has been intentionally selected.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the transcript statement that after bag out the system returns to running unless the operator chooses shutdown.

stop_or_escalate_if: The observed state or behavior suggests the system is not ready for another sort despite returning to running

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: “once it's done with bag out, it will go to a running state” and “unless you choose to put the system shut down”
The system automatically returns to running state and remains ready for another sort unless an operator chooses shutdown.

steps.title: Interpret shutdown as an intentional operator choice
steps.purpose: Clarify that shutdown is not automatic after bag out and may be chosen when operations will pause for a longer period.
steps.instruction: If the next sort will not start for a longer period, interpret the source as allowing the operator to choose shutdown rather than leaving the system in running.
steps.expected_result: The operator understands shutdown as a separate deliberate choice after bag out, not the default automatic outcome.
steps.healthy_condition: Shutdown is treated as an intentional operator-selected state when needed.
steps.failure_condition: Shutdown is assumed to be automatic or is inferred without an intentional operator choice.

actions.label: Interpret shutdown choice
actions.exact_action: If the next sort will not start for a longer period, interpret the source as allowing the operator to choose shutdown rather than leaving the system in running.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the transcript language that shutdown occurs only if the operator chooses to put the system shut down.

stop_or_escalate_if: A shutdown state appears without an intentional operator choice and does not match the training description

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: “unless you choose to put the system shut down”

steps.title: Do not assume automatic shutdown after bag out
steps.purpose: Prevent misinterpretation of the post-bag-out state by comparing the observed state to the source-backed description.
steps.instruction: Do not assume shutdown happens automatically at the end of bag out; compare the observed state to the source description.
steps.expected_result: The operator uses the training description as the reference and identifies mismatches for escalation.
steps.healthy_condition: Observed behavior matches the source description: return to running unless shutdown was chosen.
steps.failure_condition: Observed behavior differs from the source description or cannot be reconciled with it.

actions.label: Compare observed state to source
actions.exact_action: Do not assume shutdown happens automatically at the end of bag out; compare the observed state to the source description.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use this frame as visual support for the training discussion of running versus shutdown after bag out.

stop_or_escalate_if: The observed state after bag out does not match the documented training description
Further troubleshooting is needed, because the source does not provide detailed diagnosis steps

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: “once it's done with bag out, it will go to a running state” and “unless you choose to put the system shut down”
The source does not provide detailed troubleshooting if the state does not return to running.

success_criteria: Bag out or close out completion is correctly identified
The operator verifies whether the system returned to running after bag out
The operator correctly interprets that shutdown is a separate intentional choice
Observed behavior is compared against the source-backed description rather than assumed
healthy_conditions: After bag out, the system returns to running
The system is ready for another sort unless shutdown has been intentionally selected
Shutdown is treated as an operator choice rather than an automatic end-of-bag-out state
failure_conditions: The observed state after bag out does not match the training description
The system does not appear to return to running after bag out completion
Shutdown is assumed to occur automatically without evidence of an intentional choice
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the observed state after bag out does not match the documented training description.
Escalate for troubleshooting if the system does not return to running after bag out, because this source does not provide detailed diagnostic steps.

Lifecycle overview showing bag-out and shutdown as distinct phases in the broader system model.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: “once it's done with bag out, it will go to a running state” and “unless you choose to put the system shut down”

missing_details: The source does not provide exact HMI screen names or navigation paths for checking system state.
The source does not provide a detailed troubleshooting workflow if the system does not return to running.
The source does not provide a formal shutdown procedure.
The source does not provide a time estimate for this verification.
